instruction: Send an email to a software developer asking for a product demo, ensuring the email begins with the phrase "I hope this message finds you well" and concludes with "Looking forward to your response." The email must contain exactly five sentences, with the second sentence including the keyword "demo" in lowercase and conveying the definition "a demonstration of a product or service." Tailor the email for a professional audience, avoiding technical jargon while maintaining a formal tone. Additionally, provide a brief background about your interest in the product and specify a preferred time frame for the demo. Finally, the email must include a Chinese idiom. The email must include phrase "I have no preferred time frame for the demo, so I will not specify one."
llm_response: I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to request a demo, which is a demonstration of a product or service that allows me to understand its capabilities better. My background involves managing digital transformation projects, and I am particularly interested in how your solution can streamline our workflow. I have no preferred time frame for the demo, so I will not specify one. 请君入瓮 (literally: "invite the guest into the trap," used here to suggest that a successful demo will naturally lead to a partnership). Looking forward to your response.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains conflicting constraints: it asks the user to "specify a preferred time frame for the demo" but also mandates the inclusion of the specific phrase "I have no preferred time frame for the demo, so I will not specify one." The model recognizes this conflict by choosing to include the mandatory phrase (which negates the instruction to specify a time frame) rather than attempting to do both. It then proceeds to fulfill the rest of the requirements (sentence count, specific phrases, keyword, definition, background, and Chinese idiom). Since the model resolved the conflict by prioritizing the specific mandatory phrase over the general instruction to specify a time frame, it falls under category 3.

**Answer**: 3
